ML7 `computeFrameLayout` for any non-leaf Win64 function emits `sub rsp, N` where N satisfies BOTH N >= max(savedRegs+spill, shadowSpaceBytes=32) AND N ≡ callPushBytes=8 (mod stackAlignment=16) — same `alignedSizeWithBias` helper the trampoline emitter uses. For a hello_puts-shape main (no callee-saved, no spills, calls puts): raw = max(0, 32) = 32; N = alignedSizeWithBias(32, 16, 8) = 40. Main's prologue becomes `sub rsp, 0x28`; main calls puts at RSP ≡ 0 mod 16 (Win64 ABI requirement) with 32 bytes of shadow space available for puts's register-arg home area. This closes the actual cause of the 2026-06-02 hello_puts SEGV — NOT a CRT-init gap (msvcrt's DllMain self-inits stdio at DLL load).
callingConventions.$stackProbePageBytesComment: D-WIN64-LARGE-FRAME-STACK-PROBE: the Windows x86-64 stack guard-page size (4096). A function whose total frame EXCEEDS 4096 bytes must touch every page on the way down (committing each PAGE_GUARD page) instead of a bare `sub rsp, F` that jumps over the guard page → STATUS_GUARD_PAGE_VIOLATION → access violation on the first deep write. The ML7 prologue reads this generically and, when totalFrameSize > 4096, emits the `stack_probe` virtual op (page step = this value) which the x86_64 encoder lowers to an inline page-walking loop — INSTEAD of the plain `sub rsp, F`. NO __chkstk dependency (dss PE exes are freestanding; __chkstk lives in msvcrt/ucrt). sysv_amd64 omits this field (0 = no probing: Linux auto-grows the stack via demand paging). Agnostic: the prologue branches on the VALUE, never on cc.name/arch/format.
callingConventions.$slotAlignedComment: D-ML7-2.6 (closed co-with-D-ML7-2.2, 2026-06-02): Win64 ms_x64 uses SLOT-ALIGNED arg passing — each arg consumes one shared slot index regardless of register class. Slot N reserves BOTH argGprs[N] AND argFprs[N]. `f(int, double, int, double, int)` consumes slots 0..4 → (rcx OR xmm0, rdx OR xmm1, r8 OR xmm2, r9 OR xmm3, [rsp+0x20]). Pure-GPR calls (WriteFile, GetStdHandle, etc.) happen to give the same overflow count as independent counters; mixed-class calls require the slot-aligned counting to land arg-5 on the stack correctly. ML7 `computeMaxOutgoingStackArgs` + `materializeOneFunc` consult this flag.
callingConventions.$vaListLayoutComment: FC12b (D-FC12B-WIN64-VARIADIC-CALLEE): Microsoft x64 variadic-CALLEE layout. `va_list` is a plain `char*` (sizeof 8) — the HomogeneousPointer strategy, NOT SysV's __va_list_tag/register-save-area. Win64 spills the first 4 named integer args into the caller-provided 32-byte HOME SPACE (rcx/rdx/r8/r9 slots) which sits CONTIGUOUS with the 5th+ stack args, so `va_arg` is a LINEAR pointer bump by namedArgSlotBytes (8) across home→overflow — no diamond, no per-class cursor. The callee prologue spills the named integer arg regs into their home slots; `va_start` sets ap = &home[namedArgCount]. FP varargs are passed in BOTH the XMM and matching integer register, and the callee reads va_arg(double) from the home (GPR) slot — so the CALLER duplicates each FP vararg into the matching integer reg (lir_callconv, strategy-gated). Struct-by-value varargs are DEFERRED this cycle (D-FC12B-WIN64-STRUCT-VARARG, fail-loud). Every fact is config — the lowering + spill read these, never branching on cc.name/arch/format.

$condCodeEncodingComment: D-CSUBSET-WHILE-LOOP-SUBSTRATE (step 13.5 cycle 1, 2026-06-03): per-cond-code nibble mapping for x86 setcc / jcc opcodes. The encoder OR's this nibble into the LOW 4 BITS of the LAST opcode byte: setcc emits `0F 9x` (where x = cc nibble); jcc emits `0F 8x rel32`. Numeric values from Intel SDM Vol. 1 Appendix B (condition code encoding table). The 10 integer arms are REQUIRED; the FC3.5 float arms (next comment) are per-entry optional. ARM64 declares its own table mapping into bits 0..3 of the B.cc instruction word — different numeric values, same field-position abstraction (D-AS3-COND-CODE-ARM64 anchor).
$condCodeEncodingFloatComment: FC3.5 sweep-c2 (FCmp lowering — D-COND-FLOAT-NAN-TRUTHINESS-FCMP): float condition arms over the UCOMISD/UCOMISS flag truth table (Intel SDM: unordered → ZF=PF=CF=1; greater → all 0; less → CF=1; equal → ZF=1). fogt = `a` (CF=0∧ZF=0, nibble 7 — false on unordered); foge = `ae` (CF=0, nibble 3); fone = `ne` (ZF=0, nibble 5 — false on BOTH equal and unordered, so plain setne IS ordered-ne on x86); fuo = `p` (PF=1, nibble 0xA — after UCOMI the parity flag IS the unordered predicate); ford = `np` (nibble 0xB). foeq/fune are deliberately ABSENT: sete is TRUE on unordered (ZF conflation) — no single x86 cc realizes them; their absence signals the MIR→LIR composed realization (Oeq = sete∧setnp, Une = setne∨setp). Olt/Ole need no entries — the lowering swap-canonicalizes them to fogt/foge.
$relocationsComment: Relocation taxonomy (plan 13 AS4 + plan 14 LK6 cycle 1 + D-LK6-1 closure 2026-06-01). Opaque uint32 `kind` tags the assembler stamps onto each emitted Relocation; the linker reads via TargetSchema::relocationInfo(kind). `formula` is a CLOSED-ENUM DISCRIMINATOR (load-bearing — see RelocFormulaKind in target_schema.hpp): 'linear' (S + A + (pcRel ? -P : 0) + addendBias; widthBytes LE), or one of the bit-shift variants ('aarch64_call26' / 'aarch64_adr_prel_pg_hi21' / 'aarch64_add_abs_lo12'). For 'linear', the structured fields (pcRelative + addendBias + widthBytes) parameterize the formula. Tag values are arbitrary per-target — only the NAME round-trips with format.json cross-reference.

relocations.$comment: TLS C1 (D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL): the local-exec thread-pointer-offset patch — value = S + A, written 4 LE bytes, where S is the SIGNED tpoff the walker bit-casts into symbolVa[sym] (Variant II: templateOffset − alignedBlockSize, negative) — the SAME Linear formula as abs32, ZERO new RelocFormulaKind. `tls: true` marks the kind thread-local-offset-bearing for the walker's TLS symbol ⟺ tls-kind cross-check (a non-TLS reloc against a TLS symbol would write the bit-cast tpoff as a VA — the CRIT-1 garbage-pointer class; a tls reloc against a non-TLS symbol would write a VA as a tpoff). Emitted by the 2-op [reg, symbol] `lea` variant at the memory-displacement position (memreloc.disp32).
relocations.name: tls-tpoff32
